_provenance: Schema-derived from docs/research/m3-provenance/literature-pass.md §1.3, §2.3, §3.3, §3.4 + the M3 plan at feature-plans/decision-science/phase-1.5/m3-redrive-provenance-gaps/plan.md §69-72. No producer-computed values -- every field is a named enum constant, a citation requirement, or a structural shape assertion. NOT regenerated from any spec_bundle persistence code; this fixture is the spec.

r1_facet._value_note: The facet_value (or its justification) must reference the closed-form formula AND the citing paper. THEORY discipline means the curve is derived from i.i.d.-returns square-root-of-time scaling -- zero free parameters, no calibration.
r1_facet._intended_direction_note: Per literature-pass.md §1.5: 'the new R1 curve is less aggressive midday by up to ~0.45 in decay-weight, equivalent to ~0.45 pp wider stop at t=0.5, monotone-converging at endpoints; expected effect: fewer mid-morning exits, more late-afternoon exits.' Per M3 plan §118, an after-the-fact direction declaration is circular -- the declaration must be persisted with the spec_bundle BEFORE any S-1 attribution-table run.

r2_facet._value_note: R2 is a provenance-only closure per literature-pass.md §2.3 -- the runtime is byte-identical to pre-M3. The facet records the optimal-stopping regime-switch framing that justifies the gate shape; the threshold value (vwap_cross_hwm_pct) continues to live in the Optuna search space (BHY haircut surface, distinct from NN1).
r2_facet._intended_direction_note: Per literature-pass.md §2.4: 'Max-absolute-deviation: 0 (zero divergent cycles in the S-1 Stage 2 attribution table for R2).' The intended_direction is 'no behavioral change; only the provenance comment moves.' The strings 'byte-identical' and 'regime-switch' pin both halves of that claim.

freeze_discipline_constraints._note: Per M3 plan §69 + council synthesis §2.5. BACKTEST_SELECTION is the NN1-violation discipline that turns the BHY haircut into a lie by omission. OOS is the stricter frozen-eval-peek violation. Both are HARD FAIL for an M3 facet.

persistence_contract._note: impl-m3 chooses the entry point -- either extend get_or_create_phase1_theory_bundle_id to include the R1 + R2 facets, or add a sibling helper (e.g. get_or_create_phase15_m3_bundle_id). The test asserts the CONTRACT: after the entry point is called, both facets exist with the right discipline + content. The choice of entry point is impl-m3's.
